title: NLS
text: NLS Specifies the character set supported by the current Uniface installation. NLS = LanguageCode Arguments LanguageCode —character set abbreviation Table 1. NLS language codes Abbreviation Character set name USA English CEN Central European CYR Cyrillic ARB Arabic ELL Greek HEB Hebrew CHZ Simplified Chinese KOR Korean JPN Japanese CHT Traditional Chinese Default value Character set selected during Uniface installation File section [uniface_dlls] Description The Uniface installation program initializes the NLS setting to the abbreviation of the character set selected during Uniface installation (in the Character set selection screen). For example, if the selected character set is Japanese, then NLS =JPN . Although you can modify this setting, it is recommended that you keep the installed value. Furthermore, the installed character set should be the same as the language version of your Microsoft Windows installation. For example, when you install Uniface on a Japanese Windows system, select the Japanese character set. If you modify this setting, make sure that the Windows installation supports your chosen character set and that the corresponding .dll file exists in Uniface's /bin directory. For Arabic, Hebrew, Simplified Chinese, Korean, and Japanese, the .dll files are installed in the /bin directory and are named unls LAN .dll , where LanguageCode is the language abbreviation. If the .dll file does not exist, or if Microsoft Windows does not support the character set, Uniface uses NLS=USA . The NLS setting can be taken from either the local or the default .ini file.
